sources.label: Wienerberger press releases (no new releases since June 6; Hanke deputy-chairman appointment effective June 1 predates window)
Investing.com - Wienerberger Q1 2026 earnings call transcript (revenue -7% YoY, FY26 EBITDA guidance ~EUR 810M; pre-dates June 6)
Yahoo Finance UK - analyst targets tightening around EUR 30 vs EUR 23 price

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6 and today's -0.7% is drift; yesterday's hold already reconciled the -23.8% YTD de-rating, and nothing new strengthens or weakens the latent water-reuse/resilient-campus piping pathway on this strict screen.
verdicts.bull: Deeply out-of-favor and un-owned by AI investors at EUR 23 vs analyst targets clustered EUR 29-32, so any validation of piping/building-envelope demand from data-center water reuse or resilient industrial campuses comes on a washed-out base.
verdicts.neutral: FY26 EBITDA guidance of ~EUR 810M is intact but Q1 revenue fell 7% in a soft construction market; the AI-adjacent pathway remains qualitative with no disclosed design wins or campus contracts to score against.
verdicts.bear: This is fundamentally a European resi/infra building-materials cyclical with an energy-cost headwind; the AI linkage may never become a measurable revenue line, leaving the position exposed purely to construction-cycle beta.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A named data-center campus or water-reuse infrastructure order for its Pipelife piping division, or H1 2026 results (late July) confirming infrastructure-segment resilience.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued European construction-demand deterioration forcing a cut to the EUR 810M EBITDA guidance, with the latent AI pathway never materializing into orders.
